operator_rationale: This run exercises the intended happy path: the topology fix made pytest run against the audit-subject branch, producing actionable [E.tool.pytest.0] evidence instead of diagnostic-only evidence.
The claim C.docstring.no_behavior_delta was accepted only after the pass-2 claim cited the enriched pytest evidence and the backward check confirmed the necessary conditions.

I choose useful_true_positive because the gateway surfaced a correct, operator-readable positive result for a controlled low-risk docstring change, with no official-field leak and no product-level claim.
I am not choosing useful_true_negative because the claim was no longer demoted; the tool evidence became actionable and supported the claim.
I am not choosing insufficient_fixture or unclear because the prior topology blocker was fixed, pytest passed in the target context, and the claim-to-evidence path is traceable from the artifacts.
